creator: tjlegg@gmail.com
text:
diff files/patch-vio_viosslfactories.c files.orig/patch-vio_viosslfactories.c
1c1
< --- vio/viosslfactories.c.orig        2022-08-05 18:25:13 UTC
---
> --- vio/viosslfactories.c.orig        2020-05-13 20:59:55 UTC
8c8
< +#if OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x10100000L || LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x03050000f
---
> +#if OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x10100000L || defined(LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER)


diff files/patch-mysys__ssl_my__crypt.cc files.orig/patch-mysys__ssl_my__crypt.cc
1c1
< --- mysys_ssl/my_crypt.cc.orig        2022-08-05 18:25:13 UTC
---
> --- mysys_ssl/my_crypt.cc.orig        2019-05-16 05:06:00 UTC
8c8
< +#if OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x10100000L || LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x03050000f
---
> +#if OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x10100000L || defined(LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER)
17c17
< +#if OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x10100000L || LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x03050000f
---
> +#if OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x10100000L || defined(LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER)
36c36
< +#if OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x10100000L || LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x03050000f 
---
> +#if OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x10100000L || defined(LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER)
40,51d39
< @@ -378,7 +379,11 @@ int my_random_bytes(uchar *buf, int num)
<      instead of whatever random engine is currently set in OpenSSL. That way
<      we are guaranteed to have a non-blocking random.
<    */
< +#if LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER > 0x03050000f
< +  RAND_METHOD *rand = RAND_SSLeay();
< +#else
<    RAND_METHOD *rand = RAND_OpenSSL();
< +#endif // LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER
<    if (rand == NULL || rand->bytes(buf, num) != 1)
<      return MY_AES_OPENSSL_ERROR;
<    return MY_AES_OK;

and it successfully builds with LibreSSL 3.5.3. Haven't checked with OpenSSL
